etymology.description: semantic 扌(手) (shǒu, 'hand') + phonetic 考(考) (kǎo) -> action of the hand -> stir
etymology.interpretation: The character 攪 (jiǎo, 'stir') uses 手(手) (shǒu, 'hand') as the semantic component, indicating an action performed by hand. 考(考) (kǎo) provides the phonetic element, lending its sound to the character. The combination suggests using the hand to agitate or mix, leading to the meaning 'stir'.
etymology.simplification: 搅 is the simplified form of 攪, where the 扌(手) (shǒu, 'hand') radical is retained while 考(考) (kǎo) is modified to simplify its structure.

examples.chinese: 妈妈在锅里搅汤(媽媽在鍋裡攪湯)。
examples.pinyin: Māma zài guō lǐ jiǎo tāng.
examples.english: Mom is stirring the soup in the pot.

examples.chinese: 他用勺子轻轻地搅着(他用勺子輕輕地攪著)，以防汤溢出(以防湯溢出)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yòng sháozi qīngqīng de jiǎo zhe, yǐ fáng tāng yì chū.
examples.english: He gently stirs with a spoon to prevent the soup from overflowing.

examples.chinese: 烹饪时(烹飪時)，常常需要耐心地搅拌(常常需要耐心地攪拌)。
examples.pinyin: Pēngrèn shí, chángcháng xūyào nàixīn de jiǎobàn.
examples.english: When cooking, you often need to stir patiently.
